question_text: A water wave has a wavelength of 2 metres and a frequency of 0.5 Hz. Calculate the wave speed using the equation: wave speed = frequency × wavelength.
explanation: Using the equation wave speed = frequency × wavelength: wave speed = 0.5 Hz × 2 m = 1 m/s. Always check your units—frequency is in Hz (or per second) and wavelength is in metres, so the answer is in metres per second.
